metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_406
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig. 1: colocalization of BACE1 with synaptophysin at the light microscopic level.
Fig. 3: localization of BACE1 at the presynaptic within mossy fiber terminals terminal at the electron microscopic level.
Fig. 4: colocalization of BACE1 with synaptophysin around amyloid plaques
in 5XFAD transgenic mouse and human AD brains.

Note: BACE1 is localized at mossy fiber terminals. Therefore glutamatergic annotation is made.

13/4/2017 Pim
-Literal: "In addition, our data suggest that BACE1 is present in vesicles, some in close proximity to synaptic active zones, although the exact identity of BACE1-containing vesicles has yet to be determined.". Experimental description: Experiments in brain slices are from mouse. Fig. 4 shows also a staining of human brain from AD patients.

Literal M&M

"Animals
5XFAD mice have been described previously [75] and were
maintained on a B6/SJL F1 hybrid background. BACE1-/-
mice on a C57/B6 background were obtained from Jackson
Laboratories (strain #006554). Non-transgenic agematched
B6/SJL or C57/B6 mice were used as controls."

"Human immunofluorescence confocal microscopy
Human AD sections were processed using the same method
as mouse,

13/4/2017 Pim
- the strongest information is presented in Fig.3 which is on mouse tissue. This I use as the annotated species.
